questions.question: Explain why file extensions are unreliable for determining file type in malware analysis.
expectedPoints: Malware authors deliberately use misleading extensions to evade detection
Extensions can be easily changed without affecting file content
Multiple extensions can be used to trick users (e.g., document.pdf.exe)
File signature analysis provides more reliable identification

questions.question: Describe the complete process of file identification and hashing for a suspected malware sample, including the tools and techniques you would use and why each step is important.
expectedPoints: Initial assessment of file name and extension (with caution)
File signature analysis using tools like 'file' command or hex editors
Calculation of multiple hash types (MD5, SHA-256) for identification
Metadata extraction including timestamps and PE information if applicable
VirusTotal check using hashes for existing analysis
Documentation of all findings for future reference and sharing
Explanation of how each step contributes to the overall analysis process
